data.title: WHAT A SUBAGENT IS.
blocks: · Its own context window. Independent of the main session. The subagent can read 30 files; the main session sees only the
· Its own system prompt. Specialized for the subagent's role (a researcher, a reviewer, a pattern analyzer). The system pr
Its own allowed tools. The subagent's permissions can be tighter than the main session's. A research subagent might have and no Write or Edit. The narrower permissions are a safety layer.
· A short summary as its output. The main session prompts the subagent; the subagent runs; the subagent returns a structur
· The directory containing submissions (one subdirectory per student).

data.title: CLASSROOM ACTIVITY: STUDENTS DESIGN AND USE A SUBAGENT
blocks: · A research subagent for a topic they need to investigate.
· A pattern-analysis subagent for a dataset.
· A linter or test-runner subagent with narrow permissions.
· Frontmatter with name, description, allowed tools.
· System prompt that names the input, the workflow, the output format.

data.title: COMMON MISCONCEPTIONS
blocks: "Subagent = another Claude tab."
"Subagents are for complex builds only."
"More subagents = better."
"The Writer/Reviewer pattern is overkill."
"Auto memory across subagents is magic."
